summary:
Goat Funded Futures scored 65/100 in our 2026 review. The firm offers three distinct models — EOD Evaluation, Static Evaluation, and Instant Funding — each with different drawdown rules, pricing structures, and payout requirements. Traders keep 100% of the first $10,000 in profit, after which the split moves to 90%–95% depending on trader level.

Drawdown and daily loss rules vary significantly by model: EOD uses trailing EOD drawdown (3.67%–5%) with a 2.5% daily loss limit only once funded; Static uses fixed drawdown (3.33%–4.17%) with no daily loss limit; Instant uses trailing real-time drawdown (5%) with a 3% daily loss cap. Consistency rules also differ — EOD/Static use 50% during evaluation and 30% when funded, while Instant limits profit concentration to 20% per day.

All plans include access to 55 CME futures instruments and nine trading platforms including Project X, NinjaTrader, Quantower, ATAS, Volumetrica, Volsys, TradingView, VolBook, and Tradovate, with no added costs for data or software. Copy trading is allowed between your own accounts but not between different traders. Algorithmic trading / EAs are not clearly confirmed by the firm. Payout windows are fixed (5th–8th and 20th–23rd each month). Commission rates are not publicly disclosed but typically estimated at $1.50–$2.50 per round turn.
pros: Keep 100% of first $10K in profit, then 90%–95% split
Three distinct models: EOD, Static, and Instant Funding
Nine trading platforms with no data or software fees
Copy trading allowed between your own accounts
Full scaling after funding — up to ~9 mini contracts
cons: No resets for Instant Funding accounts
Consistency rules vary by model and can be restrictive
bestFor: Experienced futures traders who want flexible evaluation models with different drawdown structures, access to advanced platforms like NinjaTrader and Quantower, and the ability to keep 100% of their first $10K in profit
notIdealFor: Beginners who need structured education, traders who rely on active support, those who want reset options on Instant Funding accounts, or traders who need confirmed algorithmic trading support

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict: 65/100
verdictBlocks.body:
Goat Funded Futures scored 65/100 in our testing. The firm offers three distinct models — EOD Evaluation, Static Evaluation, and Instant Funding — each with different drawdown types, daily loss rules, and consistency requirements. The biggest advantage is keeping 100% of your first $10K in profit, paired with access to raw spreads, nine advanced futures platforms, and Instant Funding for those who want to skip evaluations.

Drawdown rules vary significantly: EOD uses trailing EOD drawdown (3.67%–5%), Static uses fixed drawdown (3.33%–4.17%), and Instant uses 5% trailing real-time drawdown. Daily loss limits also differ — Static has none, EOD applies 2.5% only once funded, and Instant has a 3% cap. Copy trading is allowed between your own accounts but not between different traders. Algorithmic trading / EAs are not clearly confirmed.

That said, the firm still has key weaknesses. There's no reset option for Instant accounts (EOD resets cost $99, Static $349), education is limited, and support responses are often slow or vague. Commission rates are not publicly disclosed, typically estimated at $1.50–$2.50 per round turn. If you already understand CME trading and want fast access to funding, GFF is worth a look. For beginners or traders who value active support, other options may offer a smoother experience.

faq.q: What is the profit split on Goat Funded Futures?
faq.a: You keep 100% of your first $10,000 in profit. After that, the split starts at 90% and can increase to 92% and eventually 95% at the GOAT Trader level. This applies across all account types — EOD, Static, and Instant Funding.

faq.q: Does Goat Funded Futures offer Instant Funding?
faq.a: Yes. The Instant Funding model requires a one-time fee ($349–$699 depending on account size) and gives you immediate access to a funded account. You need to hit a 7% profit target and complete at least 10 trading days with a minimum of 6 trades to qualify for your first payout. A 5% trailing real-time drawdown and 3% daily loss cap apply. There's no reset option if you breach the rules.

faq.q: What platforms does Goat Funded Futures support?
faq.a: GFF supports nine futures platforms: their proprietary Project X, NinjaTrader, Quantower, Volumetrica, Volsys, ATAS, TradingView, VolBook, and Tradovate. All platforms come with real-time data and no additional fees.

faq.q: Can you reset an Instant Funding account at GFF?
faq.a: No. The Instant Funding model does not allow resets. If you breach a rule, you'll need to purchase a new plan. EOD accounts can be reset for $99 and Static accounts for $349.

faq.q: Is copy trading allowed at Goat Funded Futures?
faq.a: Copy trading is allowed between your own accounts but is not allowed between different traders or via signal services. Algorithmic trading / EAs are not clearly confirmed by the firm — if automation is a key part of your strategy, confirm directly with support before purchasing.
